description.task: Write a Java program that validates and categorizes REST API URLs. Implement a URL validator that checks whether given URLs follow REST naming conventions.
instructions: isValidResourceUrl() checks if the URL contains any of the verbs: get, create, delete, update, fetch, retrieve (case-insensitive word boundary check). Returns false if found, true otherwise.
categorize() checks if URL ends with a path variable (match /\d+ or /{id} pattern) to determine if it's operating on a specific resource vs a collection.
Return proper descriptions like 'List all users', 'Get user by ID', etc.
description.hint: REST URLs represent resources (nouns), not actions (verbs). HTTP methods define the operation. /deleteUser is wrong; DELETE /users/1 is correct.
description.expectedOutput:
URL Validation:
  /users -> VALID
  /users/1 -> VALID
  /getUsers -> INVALID
  /createUser -> INVALID
  /products/5/reviews -> VALID

REST Operations:
  List all users
  Get user by ID
  Create new user
  Update user by ID
  Delete user by ID

description.task: Build an HTTP response builder that returns appropriate status codes based on operation results. Implement proper status code selection for all common REST scenarios.
instructions: ok() returns 200 OK with the given body.
created() returns 201 Created with body 'Resource created at: [resourceLocation]'.
noContent() returns 204 No Content with empty body.
notFound() returns 404 Not Found with body '[resource] not found'.
badRequest() returns 400 Bad Request with the message.
conflict() returns 409 Conflict with the message.
description.hint: Using correct HTTP status codes is critical for REST API usability. 2xx = success, 4xx = client error, 5xx = server error. Never return 200 for errors!
description.expectedOutput:
HTTP 200 OK: {"users":["Alice","Bob"]}
HTTP 201 Created: Resource created at: /users/42
HTTP 204 No Content: 
HTTP 404 Not Found: User with id=99 not found
HTTP 400 Bad Request: Email field is required
HTTP 409 Conflict: User with email already exists

description.task: Implement a simple HATEOAS (Hypermedia As Engine Of Application State) response builder that adds navigation links to REST API responses.
description.context: HATEOAS is a REST constraint where API responses include links to related actions. Spring HATEOAS library provides EntityModel and Link classes for this purpose.
instructions: In getUser(), add 4 links: self (GET /users/1), update (PUT /users/1), delete (DELETE /users/1), collection (GET /users with rel='all-users').
Output should show the user details followed by all 4 links.
description.hint: Spring HATEOAS: return EntityModel.of(user, linkTo(UserController.class).slash(id).withSelfRel()). HATEOAS makes APIs discoverable — clients follow links instead of hardcoding URLs.
